title: require check: unknown extension fails compile, not eval
description: RFC 5228 §2.6: 'The require action specifies that capabilities... must be available. If they are not, a script must be rejected.' The KNOWN list in compile() enforces this at compile time. A contributor might remove this check to make the crate 'more permissive'. This must be resisted: silently ignoring unknown require directives would run scripts that depend on unimplemented extensions, producing incorrect results that are hard to attribute.
